commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And there came a man from Baal-shalisha, and brought the man of God bread of the firstfruits, twenty loaves of barley, and full ears of grain in his sack. And he said, Give unto the people, that they may eat. Baalsalisa, 15 miles south of Diospolis, and to the north of Jerusalem. (Calmet) His scrip. Hebrew bctsiklono. Protestants, "in the husk thereof. "Carmel, means a greenish ear of corn, (Haydock) which might be rubbed in the hand, and so eaten.
